name: Hello Graph: Generate Lego Pirate Ship Cover

data.prompt:
Generate a book cover for a pirate ship built from lego
Have a big bad ship on the main cover
The ship must be built from lego bricks

Have no title or subtitle.
data.output:
A high-resolution, professional book cover image featuring an enormous, intimidating pirate ship, meticulously constructed entirely from LEGO bricks. Emphasize the authentic LEGO aesthetic: visible studs, distinct brick connections, and the characteristic matte plastic texture of the bricks. The ship appears powerful, weathered, and formidable, with intricate details like brick-built cannons protruding from its hull, tattered, brick-like sails made of LEGO elements, and a menacing brick-built skull and crossbones flag flying high, all rendered with clear LEGO construction.

The ship dominates the dynamic, vertical composition, filling the frame and creating a sense of awe and grand adventure. Use dramatic, volumetric lighting to cast strong shadows and highlights, enhancing the brick textures. The background seamlessly extends the LEGO theme: a turbulent LEGO-style sea depicted with blocky, interconnected LEGO elements forming cresting waves, meets a dramatic LEGO-style sky where clouds are also rendered as abstract, interconnected LEGO bricks. The entire scene maintains a consistent, high-fidelity brick-built theme. Absolutely no text, title, or subtitle is present on the cover.

data.prompt:
Welcome to minivagent!

This basic flow demonstrates how to turn a simple text idea into a high-quality image.

1. The Text Node enhances your rough prompt.
2. The Image Generation Node creates the visual result.
